I like to say that we offer predictable hope or predictable solutions for what's missing in someone's life. This is about as creatives I get on the slide. Make a little chart of some characteristics that you see could, could, or could be characteristics of potential coaches on your team. So I've got clients that are credible, that are financially motivated, that want a sense of community, that are altruistic. Make a little chart and put your clients into that chart, it does not have to be fancy. But a neat thing it does is it really changes that conversation from how coaching benefits you to how it benefits them. So if you go up, if I went to Harold and I know Harold is financially motivated, not that he needs money, but that he's motivated, he loves enterprise, he loves business. It's an easy conversation for me to say, Hey Harold, I have got a really cool opportunity here. Take a look at this and let me know, tell me what you think of this. This could really solve that problem if you want to start another business. And that feels good. That feels like you are really helping these people with coaching. So credibility is an awesome characteristic to look for in your potential coaches. And credibility can look a lot of different ways. We've got clients who vocationally are credible, teachers, nurses, doctors, they automatically bring a level of credibility to coaching that people listen to. You can look for large influences. If you've got bloggers, if you've got people who have a huge following on Instagram, on Facebook, if they've already grown a successful business, those are my favorite coach candidates. Some people would look at them and say, they don't need this. Guess what? I would love them on my team. I want a team full of people smarter than I am that I can learn from. I want to grow a business with partners. I want to learn from people who've done this successfully before. The second qualification I usually look at is financial motivation. And don't confuse this with financial need. Sure, there's going to be people with financial need, but financial motivation is different. Financial motivated means they could have plenty of money in the bank, but they want to make more. And that more could be to help other people. It could be to buy a bus for kids in Africa to go to school. It could be to help someone's medical bills. You don't ever know what someone's financial motivation is, but that's an important motivation to be there. Another great category is someone who has a desire for community, for time freedom, for purpose. Think about moms whose kids are going to college, empty nesters, retirees. I mean, even moms like I remember when my youngest just last year was going to real school out of preschool. I felt like I needed a little purpose because I wasn't a mom of the baby anymore. That is a real, real tangible thing that we can offer these people. We can solve this problem. Time freedom is huge too. That's worth so much money. And it's so valuable to be able to bring momma's home and daddy's home to be with their kids. Some of our clients truly want to help someone. They want that sense of purpose. They want to be able to give to others. I think that's an innate characteristic that pretty much all the coaches on Optivia have, but there are some folks that that is so they're driving characteristic. They want to do anything they can to help other people. So put these guys into that category. And then Becca mentioned something, I think with her friend Allison that I didn't have on the slide, but I really want to mention, guys, it's awesome working with your friends. If you've got friends who are clients, I've got five best friends that have been my best friends since I was in the first grade. Four of them are phobics now. One is an executive director and that all started because my best friend Anna was my date on the cruise last year on the incentive trip. She wasn't on plan. She didn't need to lose weight. She got on that cruise and got off and said, "Well, I want to do this too." And she started coaching. So the other friends kind of all followed along and, you know, as busy moms, we don't always have time to connect during the day. Now we do because we're co-workers and it's so much fun. So those friends, they've seen how this has impacted me, impacted my life, my children's life. They love my children, they love me and they want to be a part of that. So think about your friend groups. They are fantastic client or fantastic coach candidates. A concept that I thought I would kind of throw out there to y'all too is don't forget to offer up. I think we can sometimes get into a comfort level of offering coaching to those people that don't intimidate us. The people who we know may look up to us. The people who we don't get nervous asking about coaching. I would challenge you to offer up, offer up to those business leaders. Those people who you look up to, the people who make you nervous to ask about coaching are going to be some of the best partners you could possibly have. They will challenge you. They will bring you up. As my dad always said, "You want to be the little fish in the big pond?" I always do. I want to learn more from people. So all for coaching up, I also think with such a compelling compensation plan and business structure that Optavia has and the leadership with Dan and Dr. A, it's not hard to convince someone who's already a fantastic business person to join our mission because they see what an amazing opportunity this is for Optavia. That was partially me. I love enterprise. I love starting businesses. I took one look at how this was structured and I was like, "Oh, sign me up right now." It's scary and it's exciting. It's invigorating to sign those people up. Lastly, I am living proof of this exact concept. Do not disqualify anyone. We never know what's going on in someone's home. We never know what's going on in someone's heart, what's going on in someone's head. Ask questions. Love on people. Encourage them to go even deeper with themselves. I was able to secure safety for my kids through Optavia. Through the financial means that Optavia has afforded my children and I, I was able to do something that I would have never been able to do two years ago. Wasn't even sure I needed that. It was a combination of confidence. It was a combination of financial security and it felt so stinking good and no one two years ago would have ever thought that was a need in my life. So I'm that person here sitting in this space, sitting in this amazing house that I'm here with my two kids and I'm able to support them and offer them trips and an amazing life and I just encourage everyone. Don't discount anyone. Know their heart. Love their heart and let this be your vehicle to share the mission of Optavia because it can change everything for everyone. So Harold, that's about where I am and oh you know one more thing too. I do want to mention, I know I kind of mentioned it earlier, but guys, Optavia live events. This is the perfect time to plug people in, plug potential coaches in because you're going to hear lots of stories just like mine and those events, I was the same one. I did not want to go and my business shot through the roof after I went on that cruise and it is so important guys to get coaches or prospective coaches to these events because you can't understand it unless you're a part of it and it is really, really life-changing.
